an11.16:1.1: At one time, Venerable Ānanda was dwelling near Vesālī, at Beluvagāmaka.
an11.16:1.2: Now at that time Dasama the householder from Aṭṭhakanagara had arrived at Pāṭaliputta on some business.
an11.16:10.1: When this was said, Dasama the householder from Aṭṭhakanagara said this to Venerable Ānanda:
an11.16:10.2: "It is as if, *bhante* Ānanda, a man seeking one opening to treasure were to discover eleven openings to treasure all at once;
an11.16:10.3: so too, *bhante*, while seeking one door to the deathless, I have gained eleven doors to the deathless all at once, to be cultivated.
an11.16:10.4: It is as if, *bhante*, a man's house had eleven doors. If that house were on fire, he could make himself safe by any one of those doors;
an11.16:10.5: so too, *bhante*, by any one of these eleven doors to the deathless I will be able to make myself safe.
an11.16:10.6: For these wanderers of other sects will seek a teacher's fee for their teacher.
an11.16:10.7: But why should I not make an offering to Venerable Ānanda?"
an11.16:11.1: Then Dasama the householder from Aṭṭhakanagara assembled the *bhikkhu saṅgha* of Vesālī and Pāṭaliputta, and with his own hands satisfied and served them with refined foods, both hard and soft.
an11.16:11.2: And he clothed each *bhikkhu* individually with a pair of cloths, and Venerable Ānanda with a set of three robes.
an11.16:11.3: And he had a dwelling worth five hundred built for Venerable Ānanda.
an11.16:2.1: Then Dasama the householder from Aṭṭhakanagara went to the Kukkuṭa Park, to a certain *bhikkhu*; having approached, he said this to that *bhikkhu*:
an11.16:2.2: "Where, *bhante*, is Venerable Ānanda dwelling at present?
an11.16:2.3: For we wish to see Venerable Ānanda, *bhante*."
an11.16:2.4: "Householder, Venerable Ānanda is dwelling near Vesālī, at Beluvagāmaka."
an11.16:3.1: Then Dasama the householder from Aṭṭhakanagara, having completed that business in Pāṭaliputta, went to Vesālī, to Beluvagāmaka, to Venerable Ānanda; having approached, he paid homage to Venerable Ānanda and sat down to one side. Sitting to one side, Dasama the householder from Aṭṭhakanagara said this to Venerable Ānanda:
an11.16:3.2: "Is there, *bhante* Ānanda, one *dhamma* rightly explained by that *Bhagavā*, who knows and sees, the *arahant*, the perfectly awakened Buddha, where, as a *bhikkhu* dwells diligent, ardent, and resolute, his mind [*cittaṁ*], if unliberated, is liberated, or his *āsavas*, if not fully destroyed, go to full destruction, or, if he has not yet attained the unsurpassed security from bondage, he attains it?"
an11.16:3.3: "There is, householder, one *dhamma* rightly explained by that *Bhagavā*, who knows and sees, the *arahant*, the perfectly awakened Buddha, where, as a *bhikkhu* dwells diligent, ardent, and resolute, his mind [*cittaṁ*], if unliberated, is liberated, or his *āsavas*, if not fully destroyed, go to full destruction, or, if he has not yet attained the unsurpassed security from bondage, he attains it."
an11.16:4.1: "But what, *bhante* Ānanda, is that one *dhamma* rightly explained by that *Bhagavā*, who knows and sees, the *arahant*, the perfectly awakened Buddha, where, as a *bhikkhu* dwells diligent, ardent, and resolute, his mind [*cittaṁ*], if unliberated, is liberated, or his *āsavas*, if not fully destroyed, go to full destruction, or, if he has not yet attained the unsurpassed security from bondage, he attains it?"
an11.16:4.2: "Here, householder, a *bhikkhu*, quite secluded [*vivicca*] from sensual pleasures, secluded [*vivicca*] from unskillful *dhammas*, enters and dwells in the first *jhāna*, which has thought [*vitakkaṁ*] and examination [*savicāraṁ*], with *pīti* and *sukha* born of seclusion [*vivekajaṁ*].
an11.16:4.4: ‘This first *jhāna* too is constructed and intentionally produced.’
an11.16:4.5: He understands: ‘But whatever is constructed and intentionally produced is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*], subject to cessation.’
an11.16:4.6: Standing there, he reaches the destruction of the *āsavas*;
an11.16:4.7: but if he does not reach the destruction of the *āsavas*, then by that very passion for *dhamma*, by that delight in *dhamma*, with the complete destruction of the five lower fetters, he becomes one who is spontaneously reborn, one who attains final *nibbāna* there, not liable to return from that world.
an11.16:4.8: This too, householder, is one *dhamma* rightly explained by that *Bhagavā*, who knows and sees, the *arahant*, the perfectly awakened Buddha, where, as a *bhikkhu* dwells diligent, ardent, and resolute, his mind [*cittaṁ*], if unliberated, is liberated, or his *āsavas*, if not fully destroyed, go to full destruction, or, if he has not yet attained the unsurpassed security from bondage, he attains it.
an11.16:5.1: Again, householder, a *bhikkhu*, with the settling of thought [*vitakka*] and examination [*vicāra*], enters and dwells in the second *jhāna*, which has inner confidence, unification of mind [*cetaso ekodibhāvaṁ*], is without thought [*avitakkaṁ*] and without examination [*avicāraṁ*], with *pīti* and *sukha* born of *samādhi* …
an11.16:5.3: he enters and dwells in the fourth *jhāna*.
an11.16:5.5: ‘This fourth *jhāna* too is constructed and intentionally produced.’
an11.16:5.6: He understands: ‘But whatever is constructed and intentionally produced is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*], subject to cessation.’
an11.16:5.7: Standing there, he reaches the destruction of the *āsavas*;
an11.16:5.8: but if he does not reach the destruction of the *āsavas*, then by that very passion for *dhamma*, by that delight in *dhamma*, with the complete destruction of the five lower fetters, he becomes one who is spontaneously reborn, one who attains final *nibbāna* there, not liable to return from that world.
an11.16:5.9: This too, householder, is one *dhamma* rightly explained by that *Bhagavā*, who knows and sees, the *arahant*, the perfectly awakened Buddha, where, as a *bhikkhu* dwells diligent, ardent, and resolute, his mind [*cittaṁ*], if unliberated, is liberated, or his *āsavas*, if not fully destroyed, go to full destruction, or, if he has not yet attained the unsurpassed security from bondage, he attains it.
an11.16:6.1: Again, householder, a *bhikkhu* dwells pervading one direction with a mind [*cetasā*] accompanied by goodwill [*mettā*], likewise the second, likewise the third, likewise the fourth. Thus above, below, across, everywhere, in every way, he dwells pervading the whole world with a mind [*cetasā*] accompanied by goodwill [*mettā*], expansive, exalted, measureless, without hostility, without ill will; he dwells pervading it.
an11.16:6.3: ‘This liberation of mind [*cetovimutti*] by goodwill [*mettā*] too is constructed and intentionally produced.’
an11.16:6.4: He understands: ‘But whatever is constructed and intentionally produced is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*], subject to cessation.’
an11.16:6.5: Standing there, he reaches the destruction of the *āsavas*;
an11.16:6.6: but if he does not reach the destruction of the *āsavas*, then by that very passion for *dhamma*, by that delight in *dhamma*, with the complete destruction of the five lower fetters, he becomes one who is spontaneously reborn, one who attains final *nibbāna* there, not liable to return from that world.
an11.16:6.7: This too, householder, is one *dhamma* rightly explained by that *Bhagavā*, who knows and sees … or, if he has not yet attained the unsurpassed security from bondage, he attains it.
an11.16:7.1: Again, householder, a *bhikkhu* with a mind [*cetasā*] accompanied by compassion [*karuṇā*] …
an11.16:7.10: This too, householder, is one *dhamma* rightly explained by that *Bhagavā*, who knows and sees … or, if he has not yet attained the unsurpassed security from bondage, he attains it.
an11.16:7.2: with a mind [*cetasā*] accompanied by gladness [*muditā*] …
an11.16:7.3: dwells pervading one direction with a mind [*cetasā*] accompanied by equanimity [*upekkhā*], likewise the second, likewise the third, likewise the fourth.
an11.16:7.4: Thus above, below, across, everywhere, in every way, he dwells pervading the whole world with a mind [*cetasā*] accompanied by equanimity [*upekkhā*], expansive, exalted, measureless, without hostility, without ill will; he dwells pervading it.
an11.16:7.6: ‘This liberation of mind [*cetovimutti*] by equanimity [*upekkhā*] too is constructed and intentionally produced.’
an11.16:7.7: He understands: ‘But whatever is constructed and intentionally produced is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*], subject to cessation.’
an11.16:7.8: Standing there, he reaches the destruction of the *āsavas*;
an11.16:7.9: but if he does not reach the destruction of the *āsavas*, then by that very passion for *dhamma*, by that delight in *dhamma*, with the complete destruction of the five lower fetters, he becomes one who is spontaneously reborn, one who attains final *nibbāna* there, not liable to return from that world.
an11.16:8.1: Again, householder, a *bhikkhu*, with the complete transcending of perceptions of form [*rūpasaññānaṁ*], with the disappearance of perceptions of impingement, with non-attention to perceptions of diversity, thinking, ‘space is infinite,’ enters and dwells in the dimension of infinite space [*ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ*].
an11.16:8.3: ‘This attainment of the dimension of infinite space [*ākāsānañcāyatanasamāpatti*] too is constructed and intentionally produced.’
an11.16:8.4: He understands: ‘But whatever is constructed and intentionally produced is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*], subject to cessation.’
an11.16:8.5: Standing there, he reaches the destruction of the *āsavas*;
an11.16:8.6: but if he does not reach the destruction of the *āsavas*, then by that very passion for *dhamma*, by that delight in *dhamma*, with the complete destruction of the five lower fetters, he becomes one who is spontaneously reborn, one who attains final *nibbāna* there, not liable to return from that world.
an11.16:8.7: This too, householder, is one *dhamma* rightly explained by that *Bhagavā*, who knows and sees … or, if he has not yet attained the unsurpassed security from bondage, he attains it.
an11.16:9.1: Again, householder, a *bhikkhu*, completely transcending the dimension of infinite space [*ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ*], thinking, ‘consciousness [*viññāṇaṁ*] is infinite,’ enters and dwells in the dimension of infinite consciousness [*viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ*] …
an11.16:9.2: completely transcending the dimension of infinite consciousness [*viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ*], thinking, ‘there is nothing,’ he enters and dwells in the dimension of nothingness [*ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ*].
an11.16:9.4: ‘This attainment of the dimension of nothingness [*ākiñcaññāyatanasamāpatti*] too is constructed and intentionally produced.’
an11.16:9.5: He understands: ‘But whatever is constructed and intentionally produced is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*], subject to cessation.’
an11.16:9.6: Standing there, he reaches the destruction of the *āsavas*;
an11.16:9.7: but if he does not reach the destruction of the *āsavas*, then by that very passion for *dhamma*, by that delight in *dhamma*, with the complete destruction of the five lower fetters, he becomes one who is spontaneously reborn, one who attains final *nibbāna* there, not liable to return from that world.
an11.16:9.8: This too, householder, is one *dhamma* rightly explained by that *Bhagavā*, who knows and sees … or, if he has not yet attained the unsurpassed security from bondage, he attains it."
